summary: Jess Frazelle details her extensive personal infrastructure setup, which includes self-hosted Jenkins CI, a private Docker registry with vulnerability scanning, a private git server, and various automation bots. She runs everything in Docker containers across NUCs and cloud instances, with automated pipelines that generate their own automation. The post serves as a practical guide to the tools and configurations she uses, demonstrating her philosophy of building custom infrastructure for control and performance.
key_points: Self-hosted Jenkins CI with DSL plugins that automatically generate configurations for new repos
Private Docker registry with Notary for signing, Clair for CVE scanning, and custom UI
Private git server with Gitiles UI, mirroring all GitHub repositories
ghb0t automatically cleans up fork branches after PRs are merged
Everything runs in Docker containers with configs available as open source Dockerfiles
money_quotes: Yep I even generate the automation…
It's super minimal and less gross than literally every other postfix image in existence.
Well because about 4 years ago when I started using docker, Docker Hub was super slow and I came to love having my own super fast one.
I am so OCD about keeping git repos clean and this is my little helper.
I run a lot of little shitty services for a personal pastebin and other things but those are all really less cool.
My attention span for blog posts is about 5 minutes and we have runneth over so I am going to call it a day with this…
key_insight: Building and maintaining your own personal infrastructure gives you control, performance, and the satisfaction of automation that generates its own automation.
